title: [Bug]: PyPI artifact validation container cannot load native Python wheels from tmpfs
summary: The local PyPI artifact validation container installs the published package into a temporary virtual environment, but native Python wheels may fail to load when the temporary filesystem is not explicitly executable.
observed: During the issue #252 container test, the package installed from PyPI successfully, then the import smoke check failed while loading the native pydantic-core extension from the validation virtual environment.
expected: The validation container should support normal Python packages that include native extension wheels while preserving its short-lived and least-privilege runtime model.
reproduction: Run the local PyPI artifact validation script against the latest public package in a Docker environment where the validation virtual environment is created under the container temporary filesystem.
failing_test: The local command `python scripts/run-pypi-release-container-validation.py --version latest` produced a sanitized failure report showing that the install passed and the import smoke check failed while loading a native extension.
impact: The post-release artifact validation harness can report a false failure for valid PyPI artifacts that depend on native wheels. This blocks reliable release evidence for issue #252.
delivery_semantics: This bug does not affect NATS delivery or sink ACK behavior. It affects only the local post-release validation harness.
security: The fix must remain local-only, keep the validation container short-lived, keep source-tree mounts out of the import path, avoid secrets in reports, and grant only the minimum filesystem execution needed for Python native wheels.
acceptance: The validation container can import the installed PyPI package when dependencies include native Python extension wheels.
The validation container remains read-only except for the temporary filesystem used for the virtual environment and validation files.
Generated reports remain sanitized and local-only.
Focused unit tests cover the container tmpfs runtime option used by the validation harness.
tests: Add or update unit coverage for the validation container run command and rerun the local PyPI artifact validation container test.
documentation: Update the post-release validation documentation if the runtime model needs to mention executable temporary storage for native Python wheels.
closeout: Close after the validation harness imports the PyPI artifact successfully inside the short-lived container and test evidence is posted.
